subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, UN/UNHCR, Freedom House, RSF, TI, HRW, Amnesty, Crisis Group) + international press; state media EXCLUDED on governance.

Among the world's poorest countries (GDP per capita ~$486; poverty ~74%), in an acute external crisis - chronic fuel shortages, a forex squeeze with a ~100% parallel-market premium, and reserves near 1.5 months of imports.

Standout drags: a closed political space (CNDD-FDD took ~96% of seats in June 2025 amid Imbonerakure abuses), a hot rupture with Rwanda (border closed, deportations), and involvement in eastern DRC; thin offsets include an IMF anchor and a reported inflation deceleration.

economic.notes: An acute forex/fuel crisis with deficit monetization, on an IMF anchor but import-compressed.

fiscal_state.notes: Public debt ~52% of GDP at high distress risk, with deficit monetization fueling inflation; a 38-month IMF ECF underway. (IMF Apr 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: Own franc but policy dominated by deficit monetization and FX rationing - a de-facto loss of control. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Not a reserve currency, with severe forex shortages and a wide parallel premium. (IMF; HRW 2025)

inflation.notes: Spiked to ~40-45% in 2024-early 2025; the IMF reports a sharp slowdown but its monetization/FX-gap drivers leave durability uncertain. (IMF; Ecofin 2026)

banking_stability.notes: A thin, state-influenced sector strained by FX scarcity and crowding-out from deficit financing. (IMF; Coface)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Landlocked with chronic import compression from no forex; world-class nickel reserves undeveloped. (EU; IMF)

institutional.notes: A politicized judiciary, grand corruption, and weak delivery against a partly-liberalized press law.

rule_of_law.notes: A politicized judiciary and grand corruption implicating the ruling party in land/mining/customs capture (CPI ~17/100). (TI 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Limited, lagged official data, with FX/inflation figures partly IMF-reconstructed. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak delivery (~12% grid electricity access) with pervasive administrative corruption. (World Bank; OECD 2025)

press_freedom.notes: Constrained, though a July 2024 press law partly decriminalized offences; self-censorship and exile persist. (RSF; HRW 2024-25)

political_social.notes: Ruling-party hegemony with documented abuses, over a managed-but-instrumentalized ethnic cleavage.

political_stability.notes: Ruling-party hegemony looks stable short-term, but economic distress, the Rwanda standoff, and 2027 dynamics raise medium-term risk. (HRW; Crisis Group 2025)

civil_liberties.notes: 'Elections without opposition', with documented killings, disappearances, torture, and arbitrary detention. (HRW WR2025/2026; Amnesty)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Hosting 70k+ Congolese refugees (a humanitarian credit) but moving to deport Rwandan nationals amid the rupture. (UNHCR; HRW 2025)

social_cohesion.notes: A managed-but-instrumentalized Hutu/Tutsi legacy, with Imbonerakure intimidation along partisan lines. (HRW; Amnesty)

demographics.notes: Very young and high-fertility (a long-run asset) but with acute land/jobs pressure in one of the densest rural populations. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: Isolated by the Rwanda rift, involved in eastern DRC, under heavy de-facto FX controls.

alliance_reliability.notes: An EAC member isolated by the Rwanda rift and aligned loosely with DRC against Rwanda/M23 - an unpredictable partner. (Crisis Group; Reuters 2025)

conflict_involvement.notes: Border closed with Rwanda, troops involved in eastern DRC, and a RED-Tabara rebel threat - elevated interstate-escalation risk. (HRW; Africanews 2025)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Heavy de-facto FX/capital controls and import rationing, with no broad multilateral sanctions currently. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Flood/landslide exposure, a malaria-burdened health system, and among the world's lowest electrification.

climate.notes: Flooding/landslides and rain variability hit subsistence farming - high exposure, low adaptive capacity. (World Bank)

healthcare.notes: Very weak - ~5.3M malaria cases in 2025, with the RTS,S vaccine introduced as a modest positive. (WHO/Gavi 2025)

infrastructure.notes: ~12% electricity access (among the world's lowest), with chronic fuel shortages crippling transport. (OECD/World Bank 2025)

crime_safety.notes: Elevated armed robbery/carjacking and politically motivated violence. (OSAC; HRW 2025)

disaster_insurance.notes: Negligible penetration, with near-total reliance on aid for shocks. (inference, low data)

summary: Among the world's poorest countries (GDP per capita ~$486; poverty ~74%), now in an acute external crisis: chronic fuel shortages, a forex squeeze with a ~100% parallel-market premium, and reserves near one-and-a-half months of imports. Politics are effectively closed - the CNDD-FDD took virtually all seats in June 2025 amid documented Imbonerakure abuses - while a sharp rupture with Rwanda (border closed, deportations) adds interstate-conflict risk on top of involvement in eastern DRC. Thin positives (an IMF ECF anchor, a reported inflation deceleration into 2026, partial press-law liberalization, a new malaria vaccine) are fragile and policy-dependent. The skew is negative: the realistic downside outweighs constrained upside.
